key_insights.insight: Small disconnections, not major events, often lead to divorce.
key_insights.quote: Slippage is these small disconnections, small disconnections that in of themselves mean nothing. Like no single raindrops responsible for the flood.

key_insights.insight: Many relationships end because one partner feels unseen or unacknowledged.
key_insights.quote: The most obvious... it would be that you've stopped seeing her and stopped noticing her. In the list of things that are important to Stephen, that she's somewhere in the middle to bottom of that list.

key_insights.insight: Couples should regularly check in on their 'job' of maintaining the relationship.
key_insights.quote: What you're actually saying is, I want to be good at this job. How can I be good at this job? How can I stay good at it? How can I get even better at it?

key_insights.insight: One of the biggest fears in relationships is the feeling of not being worthy of love.
key_insights.quote: I think our greatest fear is that we're not worthy of love. And I think that there's a part of us that's afraid to like,poke at, what do you love about me?

key_insights.insight: It is important to destigmatize the idea of divorce; sometimes, 'happily ever after' means happily ever after separately.
key_insights.quote: I think destigmatizing divorce and seeing that, okay, sometimes it is better for people when the relationship is no longer working for both of them, and they've done the things they've tried to do to make it work, and they've decided that happily ever after means happily ever after separately. I don't think that's necessarily a bad thing.

key_insights.insight: The most authentic version of yourself will come from you, and it is best to help your partner nurture that.
key_insights.quote: I want to help you become the most authentic version of you. I can see your blind spots. You can't. That's why they're blind spots. So I'm going to help you become the most authentic version of yourself.

key_insights.insight: Avoid assuming your partner thinks and feels the same way you do, even if you are similar.
key_insights.quote: But this is why it becomes so easy to misunderstand because we go through life assuming the other person has the same perspective as we do.

key_insights.insight: Don't assume marriage will change someone's core traits; rather keep things ongoing with open communication.
key_insights.quote: One is thinking that marriage will change the other person...Thinking that marriage is going to change someone is a very dangerous assumption.

key_insights.insight: Escaping the discomfort of a conversation will increase the long-term discomfort of disconnection.
key_insights.quote: Because people just don't want temporary discomfort. Like our desire for joy versus our aversion to pain. Our aversion to pain will win every single time.

actionable_advice.advice: Implement a weekly relationship ritual: share three things you love about your partner and three areas for improvement.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set aside 5 minutes each week to write down or verbally share these points. Make sure to vary the items each week.

actionable_advice.advice: Ask your partner if they prefer advice or simply someone to listen when they share a problem.
actionable_advice.how_to: Say 'I've got a menu for you. I can just listen and tell you I love you and just be here. I can give you some solutions and how I might handle the problem...' What would you like on the menu?'

actionable_advice.advice: When discussing problems, state it in a non-accusatory way to de-escalate defenses.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of 'You're not listening,' try 'Have you noticed something has changed? Remember when we used to...?'

actionable_advice.advice: Have regular safety conversations and prenuptial agreements in place to build trust and protect the financial interests of both parties.
actionable_advice.how_to: Discuss your concerns, and ensure a legal agreement is created during a calm period in the relationship.  Consider including specific rules regarding finances and shared assets, whether for children or other family members.

actionable_advice.advice: If there's a pet in your life, create a rule set or PetNup for caretaking in the event of a separation or the pet's death.
actionable_advice.how_to: Establish a written PetNup and discuss: what to do about the pet, share medical decisions, set visitation schedules, or how to memorialize your pet.

memorable_stories: The guest recounts the story of a friend's messy, expensive divorce and how her lawyer inflated his assets during the settlement.

The guest recounts his own shameful moment as a parent when he told his young son to control himself after he started crying, realizing he was trying to protect his son from the world but ultimately invalidating his feelings.
contrarian_claims: Divorce is not necessarily a catastrophic failure, it is more realistic to be the end of one chapter.
A pre-nuptial is a signal of trust not distrust in a relationship.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - World No.1 Divorce Lawyer: This Is A Sign You’ll Divorce In 10 Years!
